User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the ClinicFlow application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

role.description: The user's role in the application (e.g., 'admin', 'user').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

UserManagement.title: UserManagement
UserManagement.description: Represents user data for management purposes.

Role.title: Role
Role.description: Defines a user role and its specific permissions for accessing different parts of the application.

id.description: The name of the role, either 'admin' or 'user'.

permissions.description: A map of feature names to boolean flags indicating access.

Income.title: Income
Income.description: Represents an income record for the clinic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Income entity.

description.description: A description of the income source.

category.description: Category of the income (e.g., consultation fees, sales).

date.description: The date the income was received.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the income record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the income record was last updated.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Represents an expense record for the clinic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

category.description: Category of the expense (e.g., rent, supplies).

date.description: The date the expense was incurred.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the expense record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the expense record was last updated.

ExpenseCategoryGroup.title: ExpenseCategoryGroup
ExpenseCategoryGroup.description: Represents a hierarchical group of expense categories.

name.description: The name of the top-level group (e.g., 'PESSOAL E ENCARGOS').
The name of the category within the group (e.g., 'BENEFICIOS').
The specific description of the category (e.g., 'VA - VALE ALIMENTACAO').

Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Represents a budget for the clinic.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Budget entity.

startDate.description: The start date of the budget period.

endDate.description: The end date of the budget period.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the budget was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the budget was last updated.

FileUpload.title: FileUpload
FileUpload.description: Represents a file uploaded by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FileUpload entity.

filename.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

storageLocation.description: The location where the file is stored (e.g., URL, path).

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the file.

uploadDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the file was uploaded.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the file upload record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the file upload record was last updated.

Contract.title: Contract
Contract.description: Represents a recurring contract for services or expenses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Contract entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created the contract.

name.description: Name or title of the contract (e.g., 'Aluguel do Consultório').

description.description: A brief description of the contract.

amount.description: The amount for fixed contracts. Not applicable for variable contracts.

expirationDate.description: The date when the contract expires.

fullCategoryPath.description: Hierarchical classification for the expense associated with the contract.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the contract was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the contract was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores role-based permissions. All users can read, but only admins can write.

params.name: roleId
params.description: The name of the role ('admin' or 'user').

definition.description: Stores hierarchical expense category groups. Readable by all, writable by admins.

params.name: groupId
params.description: The unique identifier for the expense category group.

params.name: incomeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the income record.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the expense record.

params.name: budgetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the budget record.

definition.description: Stores file upload records for a specific user. Path-based ownership (nested under the user) ensures only the user can access their file uploads.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: fileUploadId
params.description: The unique identifier for the file upload record.

definition.description: Stores recurring contracts for the clinic.

params.name: contractId
params.description: The unique identifier for the contract record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability for the ClinicFlow application. The primary strategy employed is denormalization to avoid hierarchical authorization dependencies, as described in the CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES. Each top-level collection (users, incomes, expenses, budgets, fileUploads, contracts) represents a distinct entity, enabling a homogeneous security posture. User-owned data is nested under `/users/{userId}` ensuring clear ownership. The addition of a top-level `roles` collection allows for centralized management of permissions, which can be read by all users but only written to by admins. This design makes it possible to secure `list` operations without complex filtering in rules.

Authorization Independence:
All subcollections under `/users/{userId}` inherently inherit the user's ID, removing the need for `get()` calls in security rules to verify ownership. For example, access to `/users/{userId}/incomes/{incomeId}` is directly controlled by the `userId` in the path.  This eliminates authorization dependency on the parent document and avoids breaking atomic operations.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters):
The structure facilitates secure `list` operations by ensuring that each collection contains documents with the same security requirements. For example, listing incomes under `/users/{userId}/incomes` is secure because all documents in that collection are owned by the specified user. No filtering is needed in the rules, as the path itself defines the authorization context.
